channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US citizen held by ICE despite judge seeing birth certificate
item.description: Juan Carlos Lopez-Gomez, who was born in the United States, was detained Wednesday in Florida by the state’s highway patrol and was charged with illegally entering the Sunshine State as an “unauthorized alien” under a state law that has been temporarily blocked by a judge, the Florida Phoenix news o
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The NFL Draft is one week away; 49ers, Raiders eye top-valued picks
item.description: The 2025 NFL Draft begins on April 24 and the San Francisco 49ers and the Las Vegas Raiders both have a good shot at snagging top-10 valued talent.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: Florida State gunman used deputy mom's former service weapon to kill 2 and wound 6, authorities say
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — The 20-year-old son of a sheriff’s deputy opened fire Thursday at Florida State University with his mother’s former service weapon, killing two men and wounding at least six others, investigators said.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: FSU lecturer finds broken doors in building after 2-hour shooting lockdown
item.description: Busted doors and debris were what a lecturer at Florida State University found after being locked down for hours during Thursday's mass shooting.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump has privately discussed firing Jerome Powell for months: Source
item.description: President Trump has privately discussed firing Jerome Powell for several months, a source confirms to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows 'Call on Jesus' bus explode in ball of flames along Louisiana road
item.description: Authorities are investigating after a commuter bus wrapped with the message "Call on Jesus" caught fire Wednesday, sparking a massive explosion in North Shreveport, Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Cheng, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A US man hijacks a small plane in Belize and stabs people. He is fatally shot and plane lands safely
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — A U.S. man hijacked a small plane in Belize on Thursday, stabbing two passengers and a pilot, before one of the stabbed passengers fatally shot him, officials in Belize and the United States said. The plane then landed safely.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY and JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pennsylvania senator asked child 'pornography' question during civics competition
item.description: Republican State Sen. David Argall asked the sixth grader if it was "appropriate to give pornographic magazines to kindergarteners.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: UnitedHealthcare killing suspect Luigi Mangione indicted as prosecutors push for death penalty
item.description: Luigi Mangione, the man accused of gunning down UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson outside a Manhattan hotel in December, has been indicted in a New York court on four charges including a count eligible for the death penalty.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak And Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jury selection heads into next week at Weinstein rape retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Dozens more people were screened Thursday as potential jurors for Harvey Weinstein's New York rape retrial, but the selection wrapped up for the week with several slots still to be filled.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: El Paso mom honors son during National Donate Life Month
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (KTSM) — April is National Donate Life Month, dedicated to raising awareness of donating organs and to thank those who have donated. Heyma Chavez, an El Paso mother, is honoring her son Ivan Bustamante, who died and donated his organs to three people in need.
item.dc:creator: Nigel Wickens, NewsNation

item.title: Karen Read judge sets last-minute deadline on crash reconstruction experts central to her defense
item.description: Judge Beverly Cannone set a Friday deadline for Karen Read's defense team to reveal details about crash reconstruction firm ARCCA, a key element in her upcoming murder retrial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington State University instructor arrested for alleged assault of student with Trump hat
item.description: Jay Sani, a student at Washington State University, told police he was assaulted for wearing a red hat with a slogan from President Donald Trump's campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Over 150 dogs rescued from 'extremely poor' conditions inside home
item.description: 152 dogs were rescued from a home in Ogden, Utah, where they were reportedly living in "extremely poor and overcrowded" conditions, according to the Ogden Police Department. Lt.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, KTLA 5

item.title: Menendez brothers hearing moves forward, albeit with confusion
item.description: The resentencing hearing for Eric and Lyle Menendez moved forward Thursday morning despite a motion for delay filed by Los Angeles County District Attorney Nathan Hochman.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration takes aim at Harvard's international students and tax-exempt status
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has escalated its ongoing battle with Harvard, threatening to block the university from enrolling international students, who make up 27% of the campus.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Jocelyn Gecker And Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Officials in Puerto Rico demand answers after another blackout leaves 1.4 million without power
item.description: Crews worked early Thursday to restore power to Puerto Rico after a blackout across the entire island that affected the main international airport, several hospitals and hotels filled with Easter vacationers.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Noem announces cancellation of $2.7 million in grants to Harvard
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday announced the cancellation of more than $2.7 million in grants to Harvard University amid tensions between the school and the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Power blackout hits all of Puerto Rico as residents prepare for Easter weekend
item.description: SAN JUAN, Puerto Rico (AP) — A power blackout hit all of Puerto Rico on Wednesday as the heavily Catholic U.S. territory prepared to celebrate the Easter weekend.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NY prison guards beat an inmate to death then tried to cover it up, prosecutors say
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Ten New York prison guards were charged Wednesday in connection with the fatal beating of a 22-year-old inmate last month — including two charged with murder. It's the second time a group of correctional officers in the state was indicted for a death behind bars this year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Lost toddler found miles from home by dog that guided him to safety, sheriff says
item.description: When an Arizona toddler wandered away from home Monday, a team of deputies, search and rescue volunteers and rangers began their search. In the end, it was a protective pup who track down the 2-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WGN 9

item.title: Zoom service restored after outage affects thousands
item.description: Thousands of Zoom users reported issues accessing virtual meetings Wednesday, with the site confirming shortly after noon it was investigating the issue.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's teases the return of the Snack Wrap
item.description: McDonald's has finally teased a re-release date for a once-popular menu item. Kind of. In a relatively cryptic post to X on Tuesday, the fast-food chain announced the beloved Snack Wraps would return on the 14th.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, KTLA 5

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: MrBeast fans seek refunds after Las Vegas event
item.description: A crowd of frustrated "MrBeast" fans gathered Tuesday in Las Vegas to demand refunds for a three-day experience.
item.dc:creator: Jezzamine Wolk, WGN 9

item.title: Olympic event to be played in front of iconic movie backdrop at 2028 Games, organizers say
item.description: LA28 officials have announced a where that a first-time Olympic sport will be played at a famed Hollywood location.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5

item.title: Man in tactical gear attacks officers with pepper spray at Boston-area courthouse, video shows
item.description: Police have arrested a man who they say entered a courthouse while wearing a gas mask and tactical gear and attacked several people with pepper spray.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jan. 6 defendants, allies vexed with Trump admin
item.description: The Jan. 6 defendants granted sweeping clemency by President Trump are growing increasingly vexed with his administration because they don’t believe it’s delivering on his campaign promise of payback for their prosecutions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge rejects defense that Gaudreau brothers contributed to their deaths by cycling while impaired
item.description: SALEM, N.J. (AP) — The family of NHL hockey player Johnny Gaudreau and his brother Matthew listened in pain Tuesday as lawyers debated whether the men's own drinking contributed to their deaths when they were hit by an allegedly drunken and enraged driver as they cycled at night.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: A Georgia man was arrested after federal authorities said he made numerous death threats against Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard, the Justice Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Jennifer Hudson hosts Remarkable Women for Hollywood experience
item.description: The 2025 Remarkable Women trip continues to dazzle as the winners took in a true taste of Tinseltown on Monday.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: Go inside the factory where Peeps are made
item.description: BETHLEHEM, Pa. (AP) — Love them or hate them, those marshmallow Peeps that come in blindingly bright colors and an array of flavors are inescapable around the Easter holiday.
item.dc:creator: TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal judge drops contempt case against ICE agent over arrest outside Boston courthouse
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge has dismissed a contempt case against an agent for the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement whose arrest of a man during his trial sparked criticism among the law enforcement community in Boston.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jeffries: Court should hold Trump officials in contempt over wrongly deported Maryland man
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said Tuesday that the Supreme Court should hold members of the Trump administration in contempt of court if they don’t move to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported last month to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9

item.title: Study finds more people are obtaining abortions but fewer are traveling to other states for it
item.description: Fewer people crossed state lines to obtain abortions in 2024 than a year earlier, a new survey has found.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Palou finally loses as IndyCar champ's 2-race winning streak snapped by Kirkwood in Long Beach
item.description: Kyle Kirkwood showed that Alex Palou can be beat this IndyCar season and returned Andretti Global to the top of the podium with his second career victory at the Grand Prix of Long Beach.
item.dc:creator: JENNA FRYER, Associated Press, Fox 5
